How's it going everyone? I wanted to do a quick video about the Facebook business manager. Now Facebook business manager is a way to create a special account that incorporates a specific page or pages that you're going to advertise on or use. So you do need a page for your business, a public page, and then you'll create a business manager account tied to that page. It's actually separate from your personal account. That way you don't have to worry about boosting things through your personal account or through your work or anything else that's much easier to manage. It gives you the ability to give access to other people. So if you have somebody who's handling your ads, you can send them an invite to your business manager account and they're able to go through and make those changes to ads without having to have admin access to your actual profile logging into your actual profile. Now with the business manager help page, it gives you all the information you're going to need. It tells you what to use a business manager account, what the different features and settings are because they update it quite a bit, how to handle roles and permissions if you're having other people use it. It walks you through the setup process, how to actually accept an invitation if you send it to somebody else to access it, and how to verify your business and how to add a payment method. And that gives you how to change different things about your business manager profile, even gives you some troubleshooting steps. So go through this help page. It's really useful. Facebook keeps updated all the time. That way when they do make changes to the business manager page, you'll know where to get help from. I hope you enjoyed this video and hope you have a great rest of your day.
